channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Etche Community Appeals for Revival of Abandoned Primary School
item.description: The Etche community for Rivers State don dey beg their Local Government Chairman, Hon. Njoku, to help bring life back to the Community Primary School Umuozoche for Igbodo area. The school don dey for ...

item.title: Parents pursue legal action after toddler suffers birth-related injuries at Mowbray Maternity Hospital
item.description: Three-year-old Likothemba Ncapayi has already endured four surgeries and requires two more due to complications from a traumatic birth at Mowbray Maternity Hospital in Cape Town. Her left arm remains ...

item.title: Australian Economy Expected to Avoid Recession Despite Middle East Conflict; Rate Hike Possible
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has indicated that Australia is unlikely to experience a recession as a result of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Federal Treasury modelling suggests that while growth ...

item.title: Pakistani exporters face new air cargo surcharges amid rising costs
item.description: KARACHI: Exporters in Pakistan are facing fresh challenges as air cargo charges have been hiked by Rs25–50 per kilogram due to rising operational costs. Gerry’s dnata Pakistan has introduced ad hoc ch...

item.title: Veteran Doctor Job Obwaka Detained Amid Nairobi Hospital Ownership Dispute
item.description: Kenyans have been left deeply concerned after a video surfaced showing Dr Job Obwaka, a respected 84-year-old gynaecologist, speaking about his detention at Muthaiga Police Station. The doctor, well k...

item.title: PNP Intensifies Monitoring of Gas Stations Closing Amid Soaring Fuel Prices Due to Middle East Tensions
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) is stepping up its monitoring of gasoline stations across the country that have suddenly stopped operations as fuel prices continue to rise sharply. This comes ami...

item.title: Public transport operators announce nationwide strike on March 19
item.description: Transport groups in the Philippines have announced a nationwide strike set for March 19, 2026, affecting commuters and road traffic across major cities. The protest is being organized by Piston, a wel...

item.title: Connor McDavid records three assists as Oilers defeat Predators 3-1
item.description: EDMONTON – Connor McDavid contributed three assists as the Edmonton Oilers secured a 3-1 victory against the Nashville Predators on Sunday, ending a two-game losing streak. Goals were scored by Leon D...

item.title: Implications of the 2026 VAT Threshold Increase for Small Business Deregistration
item.description: The VAT registration threshold in South Africa is set to rise to R2.3 million from 1 April 2026, which may prompt many smaller businesses to consider deregistering from VAT. While deregistration might...

item.title: How U.S. Economic Policies Affect Global Markets
item.description: The article discusses the recurring economic crises in the capitalist system, emphasizing that overproduction is a central issue. Since 1945, countries have often relied on exporting surplus goods to ...

item.title: Iron ore prices fluctuate after China eases restrictions on BHP product
item.description: Iron ore prices experienced a sharp reversal on Monday following a period of significant gains, highlighting the ongoing volatility in global commodity markets. Last week, futures for iron ore jumped ...

item.title: Suspect arrested in connection with Witness D killing, commission responds
item.description: The Madlanga Commission has expressed approval over the arrest of a suspect believed to have killed Witness D. The police task team investigating allegations from the commission achieved a key breakth...

item.title: Progress on Tanzania-Kenya Border Demarcation Reaches 75%, Set as African Model
item.description: Tanzania's Parliamentary Standing Committee on Lands, Natural Resources and Tourism recently visited the Holili area in Rombo District to review the demarcation work on the Tanzania-Kenya border. So f...

item.title: Urgent need for Nairobi to fix its sewerage system to prevent flood disasters
item.description: Nairobi continues to face perennial flooding, yet the city’s sewerage system remains largely neglected. Historical floods across Kenya, from the Nyando region to coastal areas like Mombasa and Lamu, s...

item.title: Concerns rise over celebrity use of Ozempic and health risks highlighted at Oscars 2026
item.description: The 2026 Oscars sparked fresh concerns about the use of GLP-1 weight-loss medications, such as Ozempic, Wegovy, and Mounjaro, among Hollywood stars. Originally intended for managing type 2 diabetes, t...

item.title: Michael B Jordan and Jessie Buckley Win Big at the 2026 Oscars, Timothee Chalamet Overlooked
item.description: The 98th Academy Awards at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles saw 'One Battle After Another' dominate, winning Best Picture and securing six awards in total. Director Paul Thomas Anderson earned both Be...

item.title: Comprehensive Summary of the 97th Academy Awards Winners and Key Moments
item.description: The 97th Academy Awards concluded with significant wins for Sean Baker’s 'Anora' and Brady Corbet’s 'The Brutalist'. 'Anora' won Best Picture and earned a total of five Oscars, including Best Actress ...

item.title: Canadian Animators Earn Oscars, Advocate for Streaming Support of Local Arts
item.description: Canadian animators had a remarkable showing at the Oscars this year, marking a significant achievement for the nation's film industry. 'The Girl Who Cried Pearls,' a Canadian animated short, won the O...

item.title: Australian and global markets react to Iran conflict as oil prices rise
item.description: The Australian sharemarket dropped on Monday as tensions in the Middle East continued to unsettle global markets, with oil prices climbing steadily. The S&P/ASX 200 fell 36.1 points, or 0.4 per cent, ...

item.title: Sindh High Court annuls federal ombudsperson’s decision in National Savings officer’s harassment case
item.description: The Sindh High Court (SHC), Hyderabad circuit, has nullified an earlier order issued by the Federal Ombudsman Secretariat for Protection Against Harassment of Women in the Workplace against a senior o...
